Fix: All Posts Show Updated Date After Hexo Upgrade or CI Deploy
After upgrading Hexo, every article started showing an "updated" date, even articles I hadn't touched. This post is on how to fix that — marking unmodified posts as updated is confusing for readers.
After the upgrade, every article started showing an
date — even though I hadn't actually edited any of them.
Readers shouldn't see an "Updated" stamp on articles that haven't actually been updated. Here's why it happens and how to fix it.
By default, Hexo derives the updated time from the file's last modified timestamp. When you upgrade Hexo, you usually copy the entire
folder into the new Hexo project — and that copy operation touches every file's modified time. So we need to turn off the default behaviour.

# Date / Time format
## Hexo uses Moment.js to parse and display date
## You can customize the date format as defined in
## http://momentjs.com/docs/#/displaying/format/
date_format: YYYY-MM-DD
time_format: HH:mm:ss
## updated_option supports 'mtime', 'date', 'empty'
updated_option: 'date'
## Hexo uses Moment.js to parse and display date
## You can customize the date format as defined in
: use the file's last modified time

The "Updated" timestamps disappear from articles you haven't touched — but now you have another problem: when you
update an article, the "Updated" stamp doesn't appear either. The fix is simple: add an
field manually to articles when you actually update them:
Going forward, when you edit a post, update this field manually. It's a slightly manual approach, but it avoids the common CI-related issue where every article shows as updated — because CI runs
first, which creates fresh file timestamps for everything.
